title: Blending Sounds to Read a CVC Word

steps.instruction: Look at this word: d-o-g. Say each letter's sound one at a time.
steps.explanation: Each letter in a CVC (consonant-vowel-consonant) word makes one sound. We start by figuring out each sound.

steps.instruction: Now push the sounds together slowly: /d/... /o/... /g/
steps.explanation: Blending means connecting the sounds without stopping between them. Slide from one sound to the next.

steps.instruction: Blend the sounds faster to read the word.
steps.explanation: When you blend the sounds together fast enough, you hear the word 'dog'. That is how we read CVC words!

title: Blending a CVC Word with Short E

steps.instruction: Look at this word: b-e-d. What sound does each letter make?
steps.explanation: The first letter 'b' makes /b/. The vowel 'e' makes its short sound /e/ (like in 'pet'). The last letter 'd' makes /d/.

steps.subgoalLabel: Start with the first two sounds
steps.instruction: Blend the first two sounds together: /b/ and /e/.
steps.explanation: Start by blending just the first two sounds. This gives you /be/.

steps.instruction: Now add the final sound /d/ to /be/.
steps.explanation: Adding the last sound gives us the word 'bed'. Breaking blending into two steps can make it easier.
